hypothesis: If a single score-based diffusion model trained only on metastable-state MD data is guided at inference by interpolated conditional scores (SBI) and then refined with score-aligned ascent along r_SAA = s^B − s^A, then it will generate configurations enriched for true transition states (committor ≈ 0.5 and correct barrier localization) more accurately than linear or geodesic interpolation baselines across 2D potentials, alanine dipeptide, chignolin, and a chemical reaction because the interpolated scores approximate the reactive flux and SAA focuses ascent along the underlying reaction coordinate while suppressing orthogonal motions.
